compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
## What

Rewrite the docs and module docstring that still assert the old
reset+reflog recovery design, replacing them with the union-recovery safety
invariant. Documentation-only; trivially green.

## The invariant to state

> After union recovery, every commit rebar cares about is ref-reachable; therefore
> stock `git gc` is safe by construction — it only ever collects truly unreachable
> objects.

## Scope (files)

- `docs/concurrency.md` (~165-196): rewrite the section that describes
  `reset --hard` + reflog-based recovery to the union-merge / ref-reachability model.
- `src/rebar/_store/sync.py`: rewrite the module docstring (asserts the old design).
- Note `git maintenance run` as the scale-up posture, and that git`s own ~30d
  unreachable-reflog window remains as a free backstop we no longer depend on.

## Depends on

WU-1, WU-2, WU-3 (docs describe their landed behaviour).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `docs/concurrency.md` rewritten to the union-recovery invariant (no stale reset+reflog claims)
- [ ] `sync.py` module docstring rewritten to match implemented behaviour
- [ ] Safety invariant stated explicitly
- [ ] `git maintenance` noted as scale-up; reflog noted as non-load-bearing backstop
- [ ] No remaining doc/docstring claim that the reflog is the recovery net
compiled_state.title: WU-4: Docs + safety invariant (concurrency.md + sync.py docstring)
